notes: Local via Ollama. `ollama pull qwen2.5:3b-instruct`. [independent_value: macro_off]

rawText:
The pull request titled "fix: null pointer dereference in parser" has been fetched. It was opened by user @dev1 and is currently not a draft. No labels have been assigned to it yet. The next steps could be checking the code changes, reviewing the comments if any, and deciding on whether to merge or further review based on the PR's content.

Would you like me to fetch more details such as files changed?
The pull request includes a single file change in "src/theme.ts", which has been added with 80 additions. Let's now fetch the comments on this issue to understand any discussions or feedback.

The pull request "docs: clarify the quickstart steps" has been modified in the file README.md with 10 additions and 2 deletions. I will now fetch any open issues in the repository to understand if there are any related or similar issues that need attention.

The pull request includes a new file "test/retry.test.ts" with 40 additions. No deletions were noted for this file. The next step would be to review the changes and ensure they align with our code standards and requirements.

There are no comments on the pull request "chore: bump dependencies" by user @dev5.

The PR is ready for further review. I will now fetch the repository's CODEOWNERS file to determine who should be notified about this change.

The pull request in the file "src/uploader.ts" has been updated. There are 18 additions and 5 deletions.

Let's fetch more details about this PR.

The pull request titled "Add support for cursor-based pagination" has been fetched. It was opened by user 'dev7' and is currently in the draft state with no labels assigned. The URL to view this PR is [here](https://github.com/acme/api/pull/47).
There is one open issue titled "App crashes on startup after the latest update" in the acme/desktop repository. I will now fetch more details about this issue.

The issue titled "Add support for incoming webhooks" has been fetched. It was opened by user 'reporter2' and currently has 1 comment. Here are the details:
- Title: Add support for incoming webhooks
- Body: It would help integrations.
- User who opened it: reporter2

Would you like to proceed with any specific action on this issue?
The issue titled "Documentation for the auth flow is missing" has been fetched. It seems to be unlabelled and has no comments yet. What would you like me to do with this issue?

The issue titled "Login button does nothing when clicked" has been fetched. It's currently in the triage stage and you can find it [here](https://github.com/acme/web/issues/54). The body of the issue states that no network request fires when the login button is clicked. There are 2 comments on this issue so far.

The issue titled "Please add an option to export reports to CSV" has been fetched. It is owned by the user 'reporter5'. There are no labels or comments on this issue at present. What would you like me to do with it?
The PR 61 in acme/api should be reviewed by alice as per the CODEOWNERS file.
The pattern `src/ui/` is owned by bob. We should consider adding him as a reviewer for PR 62 in acme/web.

The PR 63 in the acme/infra repository is owned by dana according to the CODEOWNERS file. Please note that this information might not cover all types of files, only those matching the pattern ".github/".
The pull request titled "Optimize the scheduler loop" by user4 can be found [here](https://github.com/acme/core/pull/64). No labels have been set for this PR.

I see you're asking about the weather in Tokyo. However, I don't have a suitable function provided to fetch current weather information. Could you please provide more details or ask about something else within the scope of available functions?

To create a generic release notes template, we need to gather information about the changes that will be included in the release. This typically includes details such as new features, bug fixes, and breaking changes.

Since you are looking for a template, let's start by creating a basic structure:

```markdown
# Release Notes

## Features
- [Feature Name]: Description of what the feature does.
  
## Bug Fixes
- [Bug Fix Name]: Description of the issue fixed and how it was resolved.
  
## Breaking Changes
- [Breaking Change Name]: Description of the change that might break existing functionality or compatibility.

## Other Changes
- [Other Change Name]: Description of any other changes made in this release.
```

You can fill in details for each section as you gather information. Would you like to proceed with creating a specific template based on some predefined categories? For example, we could create one for features, bug fixes, and breaking changes separately.
